title: anthemis rosea subsp. rosea
intro: Anthemis rosea subsp. rosea, commonly known as White Chamomile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis rosea plant, a flowering perennial native to Europe and Asia. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oil, which contains chamazulene and bisabolol, compounds with anti-inflammatory and calming effects. The health benefits of White Chamomile include soothing digestive issues, reducing anxiety and insomnia, and alleviating skin irritations and wounds due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. White Chamomile can be prepared as a tea, used in infusions, or applied topically as an oil or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
